metadata.description: Enoch contemplates the best way to protect Fitz, who is in cryo-sleep, as their ship faces danger. Meanwhile, on Zephyr One, Davis, Piper, Daisy, and Simmons coordinate as they prepare for landing in hostile territory after escaping pursuit. Their banter shows the stress and camaraderie among the team as they approach the planet D'Rillian, hoping to find answers about Fitz's whereabouts.
metadata.location: Zephyr One (S.H.I.E.L.D. quinjet / mobile command plane)

transcript.line: Apologies, Agent Fitz. We find ourselves in a bit of a conundrum. I was prepared to guard this vessel for the next years, days. But I now must calculate your best chances of survival. ♪♪ An extremely... difficult computation. Considering the circumstance.
Piper? Talk to me! Are they still on our tail? Piper!
Yeah, I hear you, Davis! Give me a second, breathe! I'm trying to cool the drive lines. Look, I ran a multi-spec scan, 360 3-D.
And? And...
Nothing. Nothing, we're clear!
Well, don't put your feet up yet. We've still got to put this thing down in unwelcome territory.
Daisy, we're on approach. Be ready.
Not my best.
Not your best, Davis!
Thanks, sidekick!

metadata.description: May persuades Mack to recruit Dr. Marcus Benson as S.H.I.E.L.D.’s new scientific advisor. Benson is resistant given his own issues with grief and alcohol, but Mack and May highlight S.H.I.E.L.D.'s need for brains as well as muscle. The conversation underlines both the urgency and the emotional cost of rebuilding after significant losses.

transcript.line: Evening, Mack.
Thought I'd find you here.
Safe bet most nights.
Not a bad ritual.
Well, taking advice from Coulson's files. He said, “Find a way to keep your feet on the ground, and stay in touch with the people we fight for.”
So this is your grounding wire?
It's one of three. Boss Man in the morning, bartender at night, Baptist church on Sundays.
Don't say you're here on S.H.I.E.L.D. business. You just like the beers they have on tap.
Well, I'm glad you joined me for one.
Actually, I'm here on S.H.I.E.L.D. business. I'm close on a Department Head.
Close? What, he stop saying no?
No. I need you to get the yes.
Name the time and place.
Here. Now.
L-Let's not bring him here, Melinda. This is my sacred space.
I thought that was the church. Anyway, he's at the end of the bar.
This the guy?
Alphonso Mackenzie, Director of S.H.I.E.L.D. You can call me Mack.
Mack, this is Dr. Marcus Benson, Director of Natural Sciences at Culver University.
You can call me whatever you want.
Andrew's favorite teacher, then colleague, then friend. And an IQ of 160 doesn't hurt.

This lady said more to me in one phone call than she did all the years that she was married to Garner. No details, of course, and, uh, well, I didn't need ‘em. I'm not gonna chase boogeymen with you guys.
And yet you drove two hours to get here?

That's because of the university. I mean, I built that department from the ground up, and now they're canceling my classes and “lightening my load.” And I can't tell if it's because of this or this. No-good spies, meddling in other people's business.
I didn't meddle. And admit it...your drinking has gotten worse.
So you did spy. I used to share a cocktail with the love of my life. But when he died, this became a way to remember him. Or forget. I don't know.

Well, I'm hoping you haven't drowned your curiosity and that it'll still get the best of you. You know, we... lost someone, too. A leader, a teacher. We don't want to forget. We've tried to fill the hole he left behind, to rebuild. But we need brains.
You're talking plural. I only have the one.

We want you to find more. S.H.I.E.L.D. Academy. Not overnight... we won't open it for a year or two... but we need you to build it from the ground up. In his name. S.H.I.E.L.D.'s second chance. And from what I can gather, yours, too.
In the meantime... boogeymen?
I'm not gonna lie. We have some pressing questions.
In fields you only theorized could exist. Benson.
This is not what I wanted. I'm too old, too fogged up for this.
You're our best option. Two of our brightest minds are in deep space. They can't make contact, and until they jump back, we need you on this or innocent people will die.
Did you say deep space?

metadata.description: On Zephyr One, Daisy and Piper discuss how Simmons is struggling after their failed lead on Fitz. Simmons, meanwhile, continues unearthing clues in the pod and discovers references to the planet Naro-Atzia, believing it's Fitz's destination. Tension arises within the group regarding what to do next as they weigh family against tactical sense. The debate intensifies when Simmons pushes to follow Fitz’s suspected trail, while others insist it's time to return to Earth and regroup.
metadata.location: Zephyr One (S.H.I.E.L.D. quinjet / mobile command plane)

transcript.line: I feel... Gut punched.
I'm sorry. I'm worried about Simmons.
She's never let doubt creep in, not even for a second. I think it's... I think it's partially because...
'Cause she'd totally lose it if she does.
Yeah.
We found a launch mechanism. Maybe there was an escape pod or something.
I appreciate the bright side. I mean, I hope so. But either way... Either way, it's time to head home. Yeah, head home and regroup.
Thank God. I mean, one more week with Davis and we will find a dead body... his.
Yes, because one more week with you and I will definitely kill myself.

You guys never wavered. You stayed on when the rest of the crew got off. Thank you. I know this is a lot more than what you guys bargained for. And I think we will all benefit from getting back to, uh, familiar territory. To loved ones. We need them. And they may need us.

metadata.description: Jaco reunites with his team after successfully crossing through a breach, updating Pax and Snowflake on Tinker’s demise and impending plans. They prepare to rendezvous at the museum, foreshadowing an imminent confrontation with S.H.I.E.L.D. This sets up the main antagonists’ coordinated movement toward their mysterious objective.

transcript.line: Didn't know if you'd get through.
You want to drop the blaster?
Safety first.
Where's Tinker?
Didn't make it.
What do you mean didn't make it? What the hell happened?
Bad crossing.
You came through something they built here? A native structure?
He turned to concrete.
Well, now he's a butterfly.
What about Sarge? He's coming soon.
Is his crossing clear?
Nope. It's a museum.
Okay, a museum. All right, well, let's get there. And blow it straight to hell.

metadata.description: Simmons discovers alien script inside the cryo chamber, confirming her theory about Fitz’s possible next destination, the planet Naro-Atzia. She tries to convince the team to follow Fitz, but they are conflicted, feeling it’s too risky. The team is on edge as Simmons becomes increasingly desperate, underlining the stakes for everyone.
metadata.location: Zephyr One (S.H.I.E.L.D. quinjet / mobile command plane)

transcript.line: Simmons, what do you got?
I found something inside the Chamber.
An engraving? What is it?
It's Aeonian script. Translated, it means “Low Temperature Suspension Chamber.”
You learned to read alien?

Of course. What have you been doing over the last year? Underneath that phrase was a word... Naro-Atzia. I've seen it before. It's a planet. Here. It must be where the Chamber was made, and that's where Fitz would go. And he was staring at it, he would've read it and headed there to... to find a way to freeze himself again.
That's deep, deep space.
No, I-I know how he thinks. That's where he'd go, and we have to follow.
Jemma, that's a long shot, even for us. I mean, this is barely a clue.
It's not much.
It's nothing. Sorry, this is...
No, I'm right. I can feel it in my gut.
Going that deep is not smart. It... We have to consider heading home and regrouping before we jump into the juicy center of the Milky Way.
No, we can't let this trail go cold! Davis, you set the coordinates, and let's get ready to...
What?
I said no. I'm sorry, but this is crazy. I want to find Fitz as much as anyone.
Wrong.
We don't even know if we can get back from that deep in space. Piper, back me up.
Look, as much as I hate to agree with Davis, he's right. Tactically, going home may be our best chance of finding Fitz.
We can restock. We can refuel.
Don't talk tactics. He's family.
Yeah, other people have family, too!
I mean, Davis has a kid, remember?
I haven't seen him in months.
Daisy, please. Come on. We both know this...
Hey, no. No, no, you don't do that. You don't make that decision without us. I'm the one who flies this thing.
Look, we are in this with you. We are all just worn very thin.
Simmons, we have to take a step back. The Zephyr is really banged up, and we don't know how many jumps we have left.
The Zephyr is banged up because everywhere we go, Quake leaves a wake of destruction!
I've explained my philosophy on that. They need to respect how strong we are.
We're down to four people!
Exactly! That is the point! They don't know that!
The Confederacy.
How did they find us?
Simmons, kill switch.
You handle electrical.
Piper, power down the compression.
I'm on it.
It's a scout?
A destroyer.
If they didn't think we were so strong, maybe they'd send smaller ships.
We weren't spotted, were we?
No, we'd be dust.
If we stay under the rings, we might go unnoticed.
We don't need to whisper. Sound can't travel in a vacuum.
It makes me feel better.

metadata.description: At the Lighthouse, Mack, May, Elena, and Dr. Benson examine Tinker, the fused victim from the anomaly. Benson notices unusual technology in Tinker’s body. Suddenly, Tinker revives and warns of "Pachakutiq" before dying again, leaving behind coordinates for the next incident. The team sets out to investigate.

transcript.line: Is it too late to turn down this job?
What do you make of it?
Well, I'd have a theory if he was encased in concrete. But he's partially made of concrete. And that's not all. He has components.
Cybernetic?
Uh, looks like. This on his neck. Tell me that's not a lens of some sort.
I got you... I got you... You can't stop it. It's coming.
Stop what? What's coming?
Pachakutiq. Pachakutiq! Wave goodbye.
Hell, Benson. You didn't detect a heartbeat?
Heartbeat?! He doesn't have a heart!
Tell me that's not a time bomb.
Bomb, no. Time, yes. And a place.
We may have our next location. Fox, put in these coordinates. 40-12-14.7 north. 85-24-40.3 west.
Ha, look at that, right on a Ley Line... Just saying.
No, you were right. Keller, take Fox with you. May, Yo-Yo, take point.
Yes, sir.
We'll get on the road. You get on the comms and let us know where we're going.
Get out of here, kid.
Time to make some ripples.

metadata.description: The Zephyr crew scrambles to avoid detection by a Confederacy destroyer. As tension mounts, Simmons has secretly programmed the ship to make a dangerous jump toward Fitz’s last known coordinates instead of returning to Earth. The decision creates division and anxiety within the crew as they are forced to accept her choice to prioritize family over safety.
metadata.location: Zephyr One (S.H.I.E.L.D. quinjet / mobile command plane)

transcript.line: We're dead meat. The Confederate ship is turning. Get ready. Looks like we've been spotted.
No. I don't think so.
Well, you've always been dumb as a bag of bricks.
I'll put money on it.
Of course you will, because if I win, we'll be dead and you won't have to pay me.
Look at you finally catching on.
Davis wins.
Hold on!
Front shielding is down!
Piper, we need to jump.
We're primed!
Hurry!
Enter the coordinates for Earth. Let's get out of here.
The coordinates are already locked in.
Simmons. I'm sorry.

metadata.description: At the Museum of Natural History, S.H.I.E.L.D. agents, including Mack, May, Elena, Fox, Keller, and Dr. Benson, set up for an anticipated attack. Jaco’s team activates a device that unleashes a powerful beam, disrupting communications and causing chaos. As Sarge arrives through a breach driving a truck, agents are stunned by his uncanny resemblance to Coulson. In a tense standoff, Sarge kills Fox and asserts he knows nothing about S.H.I.E.L.D., shocking the agents.

transcript.line: I want eyes on something.
Clear 'em out!
Avoid the perimeter.
First team, move up!
Staying clear in the next 90.
We need you to clear this area!
Hurry, folks! Move! Move!
This is gonna take a minute. And I'm 90% that a band of natives just pulled up with an axe to grind. Do you want to buy us some more time, Snowflake?
With pleasure.
Stay back!
Hold your fire!
They have him! Th-they'll kill him!
We got hostages!
My God.
May, go in stealth. I want eyes on them first, but the clock's ticking.
Please! My son!
It's okay ma'am. Come with me. Are you hurt?
Don't worry. You'll be a butterfly.
Get down!
Talk to me.
Communication's knocked out, too.
You okay?
Go. Go!
Let her go.
You...
I don't ask twice.
You're... You're from S.H.I.E.L.D.
Never heard of it.

metadata.description: Daisy, Simmons, Davis, and Piper interact with Trok, a local alien, trying to secure fuel and information. Simmons demonstrates her determination to find Fitz, using aggressive interrogation techniques. The S.H.I.E.L.D. team reveals they are searching for someone, emphasizing the urgency of their mission.

transcript.line: You have violated galactic ordinance and entered D'Rillian atmosphere without clearance. Prepared to be boarded.
He seems nice.
I'm unarmed.
Present authorization, Terran.
I don't have any. We're a little desperate for fuel, so we had no choice but to land here.

Scan her face. Check for ident on the data-streams. Foolhardy. This small aircraft isn't even worth as much as a unit of fuel. Unless you plan to work in the shafts, how do you expect to make payment?
You're gonna give it to me on the house.
Must be the daughter of a monarch, slumming it on the outer brink. Well, that's not how it works here, princess. Who is this girl? We get a name? Quake.
Give us what we want and we'll let you live to cry about it.
You want fuel, it's yours. Take what you need.
No. I mean, yes, but no. We're looking for someone.
Someone else to shake down? Or someone you want to eliminate?
We just want to bring him home.

metadata.description: Back on Earth, Mack leads S.H.I.E.L.D. through a reality distortion incident in Indiana. May directs her team and makes strategic decisions on-site as they encounter Jaco and face the challenges of engaging a mysterious enemy. After a confrontation, the team regroups and communicates the situation as Jaco escapes.

transcript.line: Runco. Quit it.
Has Agent Simmons ever explained what these readings meant?
It started after she left, and now she's light-years away.
Quiet.
That's low-level activity.
I know. Strike teams, spread out. 100 miles in each direction.
Way ahead of you. Alpha team in position.
We need someone close enough to get there in time. Bravo team?
Bravo team, in position.
There it is!
Where? Where?!
Dialing it in... Somewhere in Indiana.
It's in Castleton! Go!
That'll be us.
We're sending coordinates now. It looks like a-a park. Move! Move!
Jaco!
Hands in the air. Now.
Just say the word.
Watch the kids.
Surrender now or we will fire.
Hold on!
Everybody okay?
Yeah. All good.
Safe to say he got away.
Electronics are fried. I'll try and contact HQ for extract. Director Mackenzie will be worried.
Ah. He'll be pissed.

metadata.description: Mack reviews the Indiana incident with May, Elena, Keller, and Fox. Keller and Fox introduce the theory that the reality warps align with Earth's Ley Lines, suggesting possible locations for future events. The discussion emphasizes the team's struggle to operate with key members missing and highlights the need for new scientific leadership in S.H.I.E.L.D.

transcript.line: How could you lose him?! What good is it getting there in time if we don't take this guy down?
I had a clear shot, but then May warned me about the kids, which made it feel a lot less like a clear shot.
You did the right thing. But you were wrong for engaging in the air. We needed feet on the ground.
Or more bodies in the field? If Yo-Yo was...
I was in another jet. And remember, Mack, all you've been talking about for the past three days...
Speed.
Getting there in time.
... was speed. Try to catch the person who's creating all these... I don't even know what to call them.
“Anomalies?”
We used that word up last year.
And this is different.
Those were hallucinations coming to life.
And this is reality... distorting.
Uh, how about “reality warps?” Uh, “irregularity storms?”
Fox. You're not helping. Look, regardless, the good news is, no innocents were killed.
Not to mention Agents.
Well, that goes without saying. But it's worth saying. You got knocked around, but everyone's okay?
At least we have confirmation that it is a person causing this.
Yeah, but we don't know how or... who or what he is. We... We need workable theories, people. Anything, even stupid ideas are welcome.
Our two brightest minds are MIA. Still no contact with them?
Not in months.
Sir. Keller has a theory.
Thanks. Well, uh... we both have been looking at these locations, trying to find a connection.
Good.
This park, as well as the city bus with the holes torn through it and the lake that froze solid in the middle of the desert...
They all sit on Ley Lines. Energy lines that crisscross the Earth in a sort of pattern.

Multiple cultures have believed in some version of this... Incan, Aborigine, Native American. Maybe we can use that to narrow the search. And don't you all look at me like that. You said stupid ideas are welcome. I'm full of them.

We'd be stupid not to consider anything at this point. You two stay on it. Yo-Yo, I want you back training with May. This gunman looked like a monster, he might actually be one. So you might need to handle him.
You got it.
All right, everyone else, you know your assignments. So let's get to work. I'm late for my morning check-in.
Who does the boss got to check in with?
His boss.

metadata.description: Mack reflects on the challenges of leadership while watching and remembering Coulson's advice videos. May reassures him, providing encouragement about his role as S.H.I.E.L.D. Director. They discuss plans to commemorate the lost Coulson and mention the need to appoint a new Department Head as part of S.H.I.E.L.D.'s rebuilding efforts.

transcript.line: Take it one step at a time. It's not your job to solve every problem. Your job is to put together a team you trust and then to tell them to take it one step at a time. That's all you can do. If you think about everything you're up against all at once, you'll crack. Fury always said, a man can accomplish anything once he realizes he's a part of something bigger.
Still watching those?
I'm hesitant to stop.
He'd be proud. The job suits you. The Agents out there know it.
Yeah, well, I'll never be the Director Coulson was.
No, you're a different Director. That's not a bad thing.
I don't have to remind you what the date is tomorrow.
I'm well aware.
I'd like to commemorate the day by putting your proposal into motion. But we still have to settle on a Department Head.
I'm still working on it.
And that's all I wanted to hear. Hard to believe it's been a year. I'm glad you got a little time together.
We got more than we thought we would. I should have something for you by the end of today.
Good. You're doing a great job with the recruitment. We've got plenty of muscle. We need brains.

metadata.description: Simmons and Daisy increase pressure on Trok to extract information about the fate of Fitz's ship. Their intense tactics force Trok to confess he bought the wreckage for parts, revealing that the other half of the ship is planet-side. The team prepares to investigate further, sensing they’re close to answers but unaware of the mounting complications.

transcript.line: Your planet has 68% of the gravity we generate on this aircraft. While that makes it easier to lift ore out of the ground, it makes your cells gelatinous, your bones porous. Now, I'm no expert in D'Rillian anatomy... when I cut into your corpse, I will be... but I assume enough time under this pressure and either your brain will hemorrhage or the weight of your organs will collapse your lungs.
I don't know anything.
I'm curious which will come first.
All right. All right, what do you want?!
Our friend's ship was cut in half.
I don't know anything about it.
The ship should have been off radar, undetectable. The half we did find had a manifest.
We've met with every supplier on that manifest.
We've been to the O-2 oceans of Trinawa, rusted moons of Pyree, knowing someone must have tracked the ship and given that information to whomever attacked it.
And you, sir, have a reputation for selling traceable fuel.
No, no, I didn't...
So we want to know if you filled his ship with your fuel and then sold the tracking data and to whom.
I didn't sell traceable fuel. At least not to that ship. It was basically unmanned. I swear! I swear! I'll show you the fuel lines! I saw some fuel left behind in there.
What do you mean “you saw them”? What fuel lines?
I bought the broken half of that vessel for parts, fair and square. I didn't know anything about any attack.
The other half.
It's here.

metadata.description: As May and Elena train together, May encourages Elena to pursue a relationship with Keller, advising her to live life despite complications with Mack. The conversation explores their emotional vulnerabilities, adding depth to their characters and strengthening their rapport as colleagues and friends.

transcript.line: Good. Remember your foot position on impact. You know, your poker face needs work, too.
I need a poker face while I'm fighting?
Doesn't hurt. But I meant in the meeting today.
What are you talking about?
Agent Keller? He's obviously attracted to you. And you try hard not to laugh at his jokes. I'm betting it's mutual.
That against the rules or something?
No. That's my point. You should feel free to pursue it, have a drink with him. No doubt Mack's picking up on it, too, but you shouldn't have to protect his feelings.
That's easy to say. How can I not?
He's a grown man.
Look, I've tried everything, you know? I was willing to work on it, but...
He dove into his job 100%, locked himself in that office.
Mack didn't close the doors to avoid distractions. He just closed the doors on me.
You two have been through a lot. But now it's been a year, and if last year has taught me anything, it's that life isn't meant to be lived alone. I don't often give advice, but that's my advice.
Yeah, that was a lot of talking. Are you okay?
And besides, Keller's cute.
Not my type.
Like I said, needs work.

metadata.description: On the crash site, Simmons, Daisy, Davis, and Piper investigate the remnants of the ship but only come up with dead ends as Fitz's pod is empty. Simmons is heartbroken over the lack of new evidence, and there is palpable disappointment and fatigue among the crew, highlighting their desperation and personal stakes in the mission.

transcript.line: Complete loss of cabin pressure. It's times like this I wish we didn't change the timeline.
Well, good news is, Fitz was in cryo, so he'd be able to survive that, right?
I would think...
Over here!
Well, at least it's not a dead body.
Yeah. But it's a dead end.

metadata.description: Back at the Lighthouse, Keller and Elena discuss the complexities of their secret relationship and the need to possibly inform Mack, out of respect. Their mutual affection and anxiety about the consequences are evident, showcasing the strain placed by S.H.I.E.L.D. careers on personal lives.

transcript.line: Excuse me. Agent Rodriguez. Uh, Fox and I are eager to chase our wacky Ley Lines theory, but we need time in Control to sort through the data and we need approval from a unit commander and you're a unit commander, so...
Go ahead. There have been stranger theories that proved to be true.
Thank you.
Agent May is onto us.
Really? ‘Cause I've been playing it pretty cool. She knows?
Suspects. I may be playing it too cool.
So, you want to put on the brakes? Call off the night shift? And I was already planning to go full nerd herd this evening anyway, so...
It would probably be wise.
It's all good. You know where to find me.
We'll just... We'll just take some time.
‘Cause it's wise.
For me. It's for myself. I'm not afraid of Mack finding out.
Well, I am. He is not small. Honestly, I've too much respect for the guy. I'd much rather we tell him about it than have him stumble on it. He has enough to deal with.
Yeah, and he's just started to trust you. - You don't want to mess that up.
I absolutely do not. But it's your call, however you want to handle it. I'm more than happy to spend my nights alone.
